title: Exelixis — Contacts
html:
Exelixis Contact Map

Governing Thought

The Exelixis contact map is the least developed component of this account's context. As a mid-cap oncology biotech, Exelixis has a more compact commercial organization than large pharma clients — which means fewer but more concentrated decision-making nodes. ZoomRx has active project relationships (someone commissioned the campaign impact work, someone issued the ATU RFP, someone received the branded campaign testing proposals) but these contacts have not been formally documented. Enriching this contact map from proposal SOWs, project emails, and client-facing interactions is the single most actionable gap to close — because understanding who decides on zanzalintinib research spend determines whether ZoomRx wins or loses the current competitive bids.

Organization Structure (Partial — From Public + Project Context)

Exelixis, Inc.
  |
  |--- CEO: Michael Morrissey, PhD
  |
  |--- Commercial Organization
  |      |--- CABOMETYX (RCC, HCC, DTC) — existing field force
  |      |--- Zanzalintinib (pre-launch) — building launch infrastructure
  |      |--- Marketing / Brand Strategy — [contacts TBD]
  |      |--- Market Research / Analytics / Insights — [contacts TBD]
  |      |      (likely commissioner of ATU, campaign testing, PET)
  |      |--- Field Force / Sales Operations — [contacts TBD]
  |      |      (relevant for PET, MSL/MEE, FRM, OCE)
  |      |--- Medical Affairs / MSL Team — [contacts TBD]
  |      |      (relevant for MSL/MEE tracking)
  |
  |--- Clinical Development
  |      |--- STELLAR-301 (HCC), STELLAR-302 (RCC)
  |      |--- [Not directly relevant to MR vendor selection, but trial milestones
  |      |     drive commercial research timelines]
  |
  |--- Corporate / Investor Relations
         |--- Public financial data, earnings calls

Note: This org chart is highly incomplete and reconstructed from public information and project context. No LinkedIn research or client debrief has been conducted to map the commercial organization. This is the #1 enrichment priority.

Tier 1: Day-to-Day Contacts (Active Project Relationships)

These are contacts who have directly commissioned or manage ZoomRx work. Names and titles are not yet documented.

Zanzalintinib Unbranded Campaign Impact (Active)

Name Title Email Working Style
[TBD — populate from SOW/project emails] [TBD] [TBD] This is the person who commissioned the active campaign impact study. They are ZoomRx's most current and engaged contact at Exelixis. Understanding their role (Market Research? Brand Strategy? Analytics?) shapes the upsell path.

Zanzalintinib ATU RFP (Apr 2026)

Name Title Email Working Style
[TBD — populate from RFP document sender] [TBD] [TBD] This is the person who issued the ATU RFP. They are actively evaluating vendors. May be the same person as the campaign impact contact or a separate analytics/insights lead. The RFP sender is the primary decision-maker to influence right now.

Zanzalintinib Branded Campaign Testing (Proposal Phase)

Name Title Email Working Style
[TBD — populate from proposal correspondence] [TBD] [TBD] Received v0.1 and v0.2 of the proposal — the iteration suggests active engagement and feedback. This contact's preferences shape proposal design.

Historical Contacts (Cabozantinib Era)

Name Title Email Last Active Notes
[TBD — populate from 2019 PET SOW] [TBD] [TBD] 2019 Original PET contact. May still be at Exelixis or may have moved on. If still present, they carry institutional memory of the ZoomRx relationship.
[TBD — populate from 2023 Integrated Tracking SOW] [TBD] [TBD] 2023 Integrated Tracking contact. More recent than the PET contact.
[TBD — populate from 2024 Perxcept SOW] [TBD] [TBD] 2024 Perxcept contact. Most recent pre-zanzalintinib engagement.

Tier 2: Decision Makers (Public Leadership)

These are publicly known Exelixis leaders. ZoomRx may not have direct relationships with them, but they influence or approve market research vendor decisions.

Name Title Known From Relevance to ZoomRx
Michael Morrissey, PhD President & CEO Public (SEC filings, earnings calls) Sets company strategy. His commentary on zanzalintinib launch priorities signals where MR budgets will flow. Not a direct MR buyer, but his strategic framing shapes what the commercial team needs.
Christopher Senner EVP & CFO [UNVERIFIED — title may have changed] Public Budget authority. Exelixis's R&D and commercial investment decisions flow through finance.
[VP/SVP Commercial — name TBD] [TBD] [Populate from LinkedIn/annual report] Most likely the senior executive who approves significant MR vendor contracts. This is the person ZoomRx needs to understand for deals above $100K.
[VP/Head of Market Research or Analytics — name TBD] [TBD] [Populate from LinkedIn/annual report] The functional buyer for ATU, PET, campaign testing, and other MR services. This person likely owns or influences all ZoomRx-relevant purchasing decisions.
[VP/Head of Medical Affairs — name TBD] [TBD] [Populate from LinkedIn/annual report] Relevant if ZoomRx pursues MSL/MEE tracking. Medical Affairs manages MSL deployment and would commission medical affairs research.

Contact Map Enrichment Priority

The contact map is the most urgent gap in the Exelixis account context. The following actions should be taken immediately:

Priority 1: Extract contacts from active project documents

Source Expected Yield Action
Zanzalintinib Campaign Impact SOW Day-to-day contact, billing contact, project sponsor Review SOW and project emails
ATU RFP document (v1.0) RFP issuer, evaluation committee contacts Review RFP for sender and cc list
Branded Campaign Testing correspondence Proposal recipient, feedback provider Review email thread from v0.1 to v0.2
2024 Perxcept SOW Most recent cabozantinib-era contact Review Kimble/SharePoint project record
2023 Integrated Tracking SOW Prior contact Review Kimble/SharePoint project record
2019 Cabozantinib PET SOW Original contact Review Kimble/SharePoint project record

Priority 2: Map the commercial organization

Action Method Owner
Identify VP/SVP Commercial at Exelixis LinkedIn search + annual report review Account team
Identify Head of Market Research / Analytics LinkedIn search + project doc inference Account team
Identify Head of Medical Affairs LinkedIn search (relevant for MSL/MEE pitch) Account team
Map reporting lines from day-to-day contacts to senior leaders Client debrief + LinkedIn Account team

Priority 3: Build working-style intelligence

Action Method Owner
Document communication preferences for active contacts Capture from next 2-3 interactions Project team
Understand Exelixis's vendor evaluation process Ask during ATU RFP clarification call Account team
Map budget authority thresholds (who approves what dollar amount) Client debrief Account team
Understand internal approval timeline for zanzalintinib MR spend Client debrief Account team

Relationship Building Priorities

Immediate: Win the Active Bids

The most important relationship-building activity right now is not networking — it is winning the ATU and branded campaign testing proposals. Every interaction during the evaluation process is a relationship-building moment. The account team should:

  • Ensure proposal presentations are senior-led and demonstrate oncology expertise
  • Reference the cabozantinib PET and campaign impact work to signal institutional continuity
  • Ask clarifying questions that demonstrate understanding of the cabo-to-zanzalintinib transition challenge

Near-Term: Build the Senior Relationship

Once active contacts are identified, the account team should develop a senior-level relationship (VP Commercial or VP Market Research) to enable strategic conversations about the full zanzalintinib launch research suite — not just individual project bids.

Medium-Term: Open Medical Affairs

Exelixis's oncology MSL team is an untapped relationship. Medical Affairs contacts would be the buyers for MSL/MEE tracking — a high-value service in oncology where MSL-led scientific exchange is a primary differentiator for TKIs.

Working Style Intelligence

What We Know

Observation Source
Exelixis iterates on proposals — v0.1 to v0.2 of branded campaign testing suggests collaborative refinement SharePoint proposal files
Exelixis issues formal RFPs for ATU — structured evaluation process ATU RFP document
The company returns to ZoomRx after gaps — institutional memory is valued 7-year project history
Exelixis has adopted ZoomRx proprietary methods (Perxcept) — open to non-standard approaches Project history

What We Need to Learn

  • Does Exelixis use a preferred vendor list or procurement system?
  • What is the typical timeline from RFP to vendor selection?
  • Who are the other vendors in the ATU RFP process?
  • How centralized are MR vendor decisions (single decision-maker vs. committee)?
  • What is the budget cycle for zanzalintinib pre-launch research?
  • Does the same team manage cabozantinib MR and zanzalintinib MR, or are they separate?

[Populate from client interactions and account team debriefs.]

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
